description: Digital tab and inventory management system for the K4-Bar student dormitory bar. Go + PostgreSQL + HTMX + DaisyUI. Replaces paper tabs with per-user balances, dual pricing, and admin controls.

userStories.title: Initialize Go module and directory structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project scaffolded with the correct directory layout so all future code has a home.
acceptanceCriteria: go.mod initialized with appropriate module name

Directory structure created: cmd/server/, internal/config/, internal/auth/, internal/handler/, internal/middleware/, internal/model/, internal/store/, internal/render/, internal/mail/, migrations/, templates/layouts/, templates/pages/, templates/partials/, templates/components/, static/css/, static/js/
Empty main.go in cmd/server/ with package main and a func main() that prints 'K4-Bar Deckel starting...'
go build ./cmd/server succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create typed Config struct from environment variables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a typed configuration struct populated from environment variables so all settings are centralized.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/config/config.go defines a Config struct with fields: DatabaseURL string, ListenAddr string, DevMode bool, AdminGroup string, StaticDir string, TemplateDir string

config.Load() function reads from env vars: DATABASE_URL, LISTEN_ADDR (default :8080), DEV_MODE (default false), ADMIN_GROUP (default admin), STATIC_DIR (default ./static), TEMPLATE_DIR (default ./templates)
Returns an error if DATABASE_URL is empty
go build ./cmd/server succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Set up main.go with graceful shutdown
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the entry point to start an HTTP server with graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT.
acceptanceCriteria: main.go calls config.Load(), creates an http.Server with the configured listen address
Registers a temporary GET /healthz handler returning 200 OK with body 'ok'
Listens for SIGTERM and SIGINT signals
On signal: calls server.Shutdown(ctx) with a 30-second timeout, then exits
Logs startup message with listen address
go build ./cmd/server succeeds and server starts (without DB for now)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Set up PostgreSQL connection pool with pgxpool
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a database connection pool so the app can query PostgreSQL.
acceptanceCriteria: go get github.com/jackc/pgx/v5 added to dependencies
main.go creates a pgxpool.Pool from config.DatabaseURL
Pool is closed on shutdown (deferred or in shutdown sequence)
/healthz endpoint now pings the database and returns 503 if unreachable
App starts successfully with a valid DATABASE_URL pointing to a running PostgreSQL
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Set up goose migrations with embedded SQL
userStories.description: As a developer, I need database migrations that run automatically on startup so the schema is always up to date.
acceptanceCriteria: go get github.com/pressly/goose/v3 added to dependencies
migrations/ directory contains a placeholder file
main.go embeds migrations/*.sql via //go:embed
On startup, goose.Up() runs all pending migrations before the server starts
If migration fails, the app exits with a non-zero code and logs the error
App starts cleanly with an empty migrations directory
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create initial database schema migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the core database tables so the app can store users, categories, items, transactions, and settings.
acceptanceCriteria: migrations/001_initial_schema.sql created with -- +goose Up and -- +goose Down sections

Creates users table: id BIGSERIAL PK, email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, full_name TEXT, given_name TEXT, family_name TEXT, is_barteamer BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE, is_admin BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE, spending_limit_disabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ
Creates categories table: id BIGSERIAL PK, name TEXT NOT NULL, sort_order INTEGER DEFAULT 0, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ

Creates items table: id BIGSERIAL PK, category_id BIGINT FK to categories, name TEXT NOT NULL, price_barteamer BIGINT NOT NULL, price_helfer BIGINT NOT NULL, sort_order INTEGER DEFAULT 0, deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ

Creates transactions table: id BIGSERIAL PK, user_id BIGINT FK to users, amount BIGINT NOT NULL, item_title TEXT, unit_price BIGINT, quantity INTEGER, description TEXT, type TEXT DEFAULT 'purchase', cancelled_at TIMESTAMPTZ, cancels_id BIGINT FK to transactions, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ

Creates settings singleton table with all fields from spec (warning_limit, hard_spending_limit, hard_limit_enabled, custom_tx_min, custom_tx_max, max_item_quantity, cancellation_minutes, pagination_size, smtp_host, smtp_port, smtp_user, smtp_password, smtp_from, email_template, updated_at), seeded with INSERT INTO settings (id) VALUES (1)
Creates indexes: idx_transactions_user_id, idx_transactions_created_at, idx_transactions_type, idx_items_category_id, idx_items_deleted_at (partial WHERE deleted_at IS NULL)
Down migration drops all tables
App starts and migration runs successfully; tables exist in database
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define domain model types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Go structs for all domain entities so they can be used across the app.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/model/models.go defines: User, Category, Item, Transaction, Settings structs

User has fields: ID int64, Email string, FullName string, GivenName string, FamilyName string, IsBarteamer bool, IsAdmin bool, IsActive bool, SpendingLimitDisabled bool, CreatedAt time.Time, UpdatedAt time.Time
Category has: ID int64, Name string, SortOrder int, CreatedAt time.Time
Item has: ID int64, CategoryID int64, Name string, PriceBarteamer int64, PriceHelfer int64, SortOrder int, DeletedAt *time.Time, CreatedAt time.Time, UpdatedAt time.Time

Transaction has: ID int64, UserID int64, Amount int64, ItemTitle *string, UnitPrice *int64, Quantity *int, Description *string, Type string, CancelledAt *time.Time, CancelsID *int64, CreatedAt time.Time
Settings has all fields from spec Section 6.2 including SMTP fields and email_template
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

Store struct holds a *pgxpool.Pool
New(pool *pgxpool.Pool) *Store constructor
WithTx(ctx, fn func(pgx.Tx) error) error method: begins transaction, calls fn, rolls back on error, commits on success

userStories.title: Build template renderer with Page/Fragment/AppendOOB
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a rendering system that handles full pages, HTMX partials, and out-of-band swaps.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/render/render.go defines Renderer struct with templates map[string]*template.Template and isDev bool
New(templateDir string, isDev bool, funcMap template.FuncMap) (*Renderer, error) parses and caches all templates at startup
Page(w, r, name, data) renders full layout+page for normal requests, or just the partial for HX-Request: true
Fragment(w, r, name, data) renders a named template fragment
AppendOOB(w, name, data) appends an OOB-swapped fragment to the response
isHTMX(r) helper checks HX-Request header
In DEV_MODE, templates are re-parsed on every request
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement user store queries
userStories.description: As a developer, I need database queries for user CRUD so the auth middleware can look up and create users.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/store/user.go implements GetUserByEmail(ctx, dbtx, email) (*model.User, error) returning nil + ErrNotFound if missing
CreateUser(ctx, dbtx, *model.User) (*model.User, error) inserts and returns with generated ID
UpdateUserProfile(ctx, dbtx, id, fullName, givenName, familyName, isAdmin) error updates profile fields + updated_at
GetUserBalance(ctx, dbtx, userID) (int64, error) runs SELECT COALESCE(SUM(amount), 0) FROM transactions WHERE user_id = $1 AND cancelled_at IS NULL
GetUserRank(ctx, dbtx, userID) (rank int, total int, error) implements rank query from spec Section 11.5
GetAllBalancesSum(ctx, dbtx) (int64, error) sums active user balances from spec Section 11.6
All queries use parameterized statements
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement auth middleware (header parsing + JWT decoding)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need middleware that extracts the authenticated user from oauth2-proxy headers and enriches from the database.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/auth/middleware.go implements Middleware(store, adminGroup) func(http.Handler) http.Handler
Reads X-Forwarded-Email header; if empty returns 401 (or HX-Redirect: / for HTMX requests)
Reads X-Forwarded-Groups header, splits by comma, checks if adminGroup is present to set IsAdmin
Reads X-Forwarded-Access-Token, base64-decodes JWT payload (no signature verification), extracts name, given_name, family_name claims
Looks up user by email in DB; if not found, auto-creates with defaults (balance 0, Helfer, active)
Updates user profile fields (name, admin status) on every request (upsert pattern)
Populates RequestUser with DB data (balance, barteamer status, active status) and OIDC data
Stores RequestUser in request context
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement deactivated user blocking
userStories.description: As a user who has been deactivated, I should see a block page instead of the app so I know my account is disabled.
acceptanceCriteria: Auth middleware checks is_active from DB
If is_active=false: renders a full page with message 'Dein Konto ist deaktiviert. Bitte wende dich an einen Admin.'
No further handlers are called; response is returned immediately
Applies to all routes (normal + HTMX)

userStories.title: Implement CSRF middleware
userStories.description: As a developer, I need CSRF protection so mutating requests cannot be forged.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/middleware/csrf.go implements CSRF middleware
Generates a random token per session, stores it in an encrypted HttpOnly cookie
On GET requests: makes token available for templates via context or response header
On POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH: validates X-CSRF-Token header matches the cookie token
Returns 403 with error message on mismatch
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement RequireAdmin middleware

Registers /healthz outside middleware (no auth needed)
Registers /static/ file server outside auth middleware
Registers placeholder routes: GET /menu (base), GET /profile (base), GET /transactions (base), GET /admin/users (adminOnly)
All middleware chains execute in correct order

userStories.title: Implement place order handler with spending limit check
userStories.description: As a user, I want to confirm an order so the drink is booked to my tab with balance and limit checks.
acceptanceCriteria: Handler.PlaceOrder(w, r) error registered at POST /menu/order
Parses item_id and quantity from form data
Validates: item exists and is active, quantity is 1..max_item_quantity
Determines correct price tier based on user IsBarteamer status
Computes amount = -(unit_price x quantity)
Within a DB transaction: fetches current balance with FOR UPDATE row locking
If hard limit enabled AND user not exempt: blocks if balance already <= hard_limit; allows with warning if projected_balance <= hard_limit; allows normally otherwise
Inserts transaction with type=purchase, frozen item_title, unit_price, quantity, amount
Response: toast (Bestellung gebucht! or warning) + OOB #header-stats update + close modal

GetTotalDeposits(ctx, dbtx, from, to) (int64, error) sum of deposit transactions

userStories.title: Implement session expiry handling for HTMX

Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create multi-stage Dockerfile
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Docker build that generates CSS, compiles Go, and produces a minimal runtime image.
acceptanceCriteria: Dockerfile with 3 stages: Stage 1 (tailwind) Alpine downloads Tailwind Standalone CLI and generates styles.css from templates; Stage 2 (builder) Go Alpine builds Go binary with CGO_ENABLED=0 -ldflags='-s -w'; Stage 3 (runtime) minimal Alpine with ca-certificates and tzdata copies binary + static + templates
TARGETARCH ARG for multi-architecture support
Final image exposes port 8080
docker build . succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create docker-compose.yml
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a docker-compose setup for local development and deployment.
acceptanceCriteria: docker-compose.yml defines 3 services: app (builds from Dockerfile, connects to db, env vars configured, healthcheck using /healthz), db (PostgreSQL 16 Alpine with credentials, volume for persistence, healthcheck), oauth2-proxy (placeholder config with header forwarding notes)
app depends on db with condition: service_healthy
Volume pgdata for database persistence
docker-compose up db starts PostgreSQL successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement content-hashed CSS filename
userStories.description: As a developer, I need CSS filenames to include a content hash so browsers always fetch fresh CSS after deployments.
acceptanceCriteria: At build time or startup, compute hash of styles.css content
Rename or serve CSS with hashed filename (e.g. styles.a3f8b2c1.css)
Base layout template references the hashed filename
Static file server serves the hashed file with immutable cache headers
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Input validation audit
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify all user inputs are properly validated server-side.
acceptanceCriteria: All price inputs: parsed from Euro string to cents, reject non-numeric, reject negative where inappropriate
All quantity inputs: integer, within min/max bounds
All text inputs: trimmed, checked for empty where required, reasonable length limits
All ID params: parsed as int64, checked for existence in DB
Amount inputs accept both comma and period as decimal separator
No raw user input ever reaches SQL (all parameterized via pgx)
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Security audit - XSS, CSRF, SQL injection, role enforcement
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify the app is secure against common web vulnerabilities.
acceptanceCriteria: All template output uses html/template auto-escaping (no template.HTML for user data)
CSRF token validated on all POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH requests
All SQL uses parameterized queries via pgx (no string concatenation)
All admin routes protected by RequireAdmin middleware
Admin UI elements are not rendered for non-admins (no CSS-only hiding)
Deactivated users blocked at middleware level
No secrets in client-side code or HTML comments
Typecheck passes
